title: loliolum subulatum
intro: Loliolum subulatum, also known as creeping wild rue, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Loliolum subulatum include its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Loliolum subulatum can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
